name: Walleyes in Red Wine Sauce
recipeIngredient: Salt and fresh pepper                     1 tablespoon Tomato puree
1 cup Boiling water                       6 tablespoons Olive oil
2 tablespoons Flour for dredging           3 tablespoons Chopped parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. Salt and pepper the fish and dredge in flour. Shake off excess.
2. In a heavy saucepan, saute the onion in 2 tablespoons olive oil until translucent. Add the garlic and saute another minute. Stir in 2 tablespoons flour, blend well, and cook for a minute or two.
3. Turn off heat and slowly pour in the wine and boiling water; whisk thoroughly. Return to heat and whisk as the sauce thickens.
4. Add bay leaves, thyme, and tomato puree. Let simmer while you sear the fish.
5. In a pan large enough to hold the 4 walleyes in one layer, heat the remaining oil. When hot but not smoking, quickly sear the fish on each side.
6. Pour the red wine sauce over the fish. Turn off heat and cover. Let simmer for about 15 minutes.
7. Just before serving, remove the bay leaves. Adjust seasoning as needed (the sauce will probably need more salt and pepper). Toss in the capers and sprinkle with chopped parsley on top.
